Artist Statement 1:
Personally, I don't believe simply saying "Oh my god" or texting "omg" is taking the LORD's name in vain.

"God" isn't His name.

His name is YHWH

He is Yahweh

He is the great I AM

He is Yeshua

He isn't just a title to be remembered,

He is a Father to be known.

What I believe taking our God's name in vain, and blaspheming the Holy Spirit is believers who acknowledge Jesus with their lips, and then completely disregard Him with their lifestyle.

It's those who say "God said this"

And have yet to have a conversation with Him.

It's those that use the word of God to elevate themselves and their agenda,

so that they become greater while everyone around them becomes less.

That is what I believe is taking the holy name of YHWH and denying it.

May we only do what we see the Father do.

May we say what we have heard Him say.

May we get so close that we never miss a word.

May it be so hidden in our hearts that we might not sin against you.

LORD, may our "God said" never be like a quenching arrow to your Holy Spirit.

But let it forever be a sword penetrating our very souls and spirit.

You are never far from conversation.

Permit me to speak, by first speaking with you.

(Anthony Gurrola)
excerpt:
In a bustling city inhabited entirely by dogs, a particularly adorable puppy named Nitro was born. According to stories told by his canine mother, their lineage once served a human master. This master was a kind and diligent teacher who imparted essential skills such as fetching, sitting, and refraining from begging. He groomed them meticulously, preparing them for a life worthy of residing in his house someday.

The master had to leave to construct a home large enough to accommodate all his dogs. Before leaving, he entrusted them with the responsibility of preserving the knowledge he had shared and passing it on to their offspring. This was to ensure they would be ready for his eventual return.

However, as Nitro matured, he observed that the other dogs were neglecting their duties. They fought, rolled in dirt, and ate poo--behaviors far from what was expected of them to be ready for the master's house.

Nitro wanted to be liked, so he began doing these things with them. He became dirty and his fur tangled with burrs. His behavior grew unruly, and he soon forgot how to follow the teachings from his youth, much to his mother's dismay. Yet, since everyone else was doing it, he continued down this path.

One fateful day, while playing near some cliffs with his friends, Nitro faced a perilous situation. His mother, upon discovering their dangerous location, rushed to warn him. As she arrived, Nitro was in the midst of leaping to a lower ledge, egged on by the other unruly puppies. His mother's arrival was just in time to see him make the jump but not to prevent the near disaster that followed. Nitro missed the ledge, clinging precariously by his paws.

In a heroic effort, his mother leaped down to stabilize him. As Nitro scrambled up, his mother, knowing the ledge couldn't support them both, made a heartbreaking decision. She scooted back, sacrificing herself to save him. As she fell to her demise, her last words to Nitro were a plea to be ready for the master's return. Nitro was left devastated by the loss, his grief compounded by the indifference of the other dogs.

For a while, Nitro withdrew, observing his former friends from a distance. Their foolish antics now seemed utterly pointless to him. During this time of reflection, he encountered a young boy who claimed his father, the master, had sent him to prepare the dogs for their return to the house. The boy offered to train and groom Nitro, promising it would lead him to a better life, though it would not always be easy.

Gradually, Nitro allowed the boy to care for him. Despite initial challenges, including resisting temptations to revert to his old ways, Nitro began to see improvements. He became cleaner, his fur groomed to a soft sheen, and he learned to conduct himself with the decorum his original master had taught.

One day, Nitro saw his old friends freely enjoying their untamed life. The boy, understanding Nitro's conflict, warned him of the risks of returning to that lifestyle, especially if the master were to return unexpectedly. Despite knowing the dangers, Nitro ventured back to his old group one night, only to be reminded of their harsh and unclean way of life. He ended the night injured and dirty, regretting his decision.

The boy found him and, despite Nitro's sorry state, welcomed him back with open arms, reaffirming his love and dedication. He spent days nursing Nitro back to health, rekindling their bond stronger than before.

Years passed, and the master finally returned. He was impressed by Nitro's transformation and readiness, in contrast to the other dogs who failed to recognize his son's call or adhere to his commands. Nitro, having internalized the lessons and love from both the boy and his mother, was chosen to accompany the master and his son to their new home.

As he walked alongside the master and his son, Nitro felt an overwhelming sense of belonging and love, knowing he had truly found his place in the world. His journey from the reckless, misguided youth to a beloved, well-mannered dog was complete, marked by the enduring love of his family and the guidance of his human friends.
